description.task: Implement Dijkstra's algorithm to find the shortest weighted path from a source to all vertices in a non-negative weighted directed graph. Use a PriorityQueue (min-heap) for efficiency.
instructions: Inside the edge relaxation: if(dist[u]+w<dist[v]){dist[v]=dist[u]+w; pq.offer(new int[]{dist[v],v});}
The 'stale entry' check (if d > dist[u] continue) is already provided
Edges from 0: 0→1(4), 0→2(1); then 2→1(2) makes dist[1]=3; 1→3(1) makes dist[3]=4; 3→4(3) makes dist[4]=7
Do not modify the algorithm structure — only add the relaxation logic

description.task: Implement the Bellman-Ford algorithm that handles negative edge weights and detects negative weight cycles. Perform V-1 relaxations, then check for negative cycles.
instructions: Same relaxation check in hasNegativeCycle: if condition holds, return true
Negative cycle {0→1(1), 1→2(-3), 2→0(1)}: total weight=-1, so hasNegativeCycle=true
description.hint: if(dist[u]!=Integer.MAX_VALUE&&dist[u]+w<dist[v]) dist[v]=dist[u]+w; Same pattern in hasNegativeCycle but return true instead of updating.
description.expectedOutput:
0->0: 0
0->1: 4
0->2: 5
0->3: 2
0->4: 5
Negative cycle: false
Neg cycle (cycle graph): true

description.task: Implement the Floyd-Warshall algorithm to compute shortest paths between all pairs of vertices in a weighted directed graph. Initialize a distance matrix and apply the DP recurrence using each vertex as an intermediate.
instructions: Triple loop: for k in 0..V-1; for i in 0..V-1; for j in 0..V-1: if dist[i][k]+dist[k][j] < dist[i][j], update dist[i][j]
Use INF=Integer.MAX_VALUE/2 to avoid overflow when adding
The initialization copies graph to dist — do not change it
Expected dist matrix for the given graph after Floyd-Warshall
description.hint: for(int k=0;k<V;k++) for(int i=0;i<V;i++) for(int j=0;j<V;j++) if(dist[i][k]+dist[k][j]<dist[i][j]) dist[i][j]=dist[i][k]+dist[k][j];
